title: Privacy tests need negative sink assertions
description: A privacy test should assert that disallowed personal data is absent from logs, exports, analytics, prompts, or deletion remnants.
body: Use when writing validation for minimization, redaction, deletion, or export changes. Do not count only happy-path feature success as privacy validation. Actions: include fixtures with representative personal fields and assert absence from unauthorized sinks. Validation signals include explicit no-PII assertions and cleanup checks. Risk boundary: high-risk changes without negative sink tests need human acceptance of residual risk. Use when Privacy Risk Controls work touches personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: use the fact to constrain the change and test plan; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
